zh.lifeSummary: 當Esteban Illán de San Román Bermey de Toledo於大約1127年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多時，他的父親Illán Pérez de San Román Alguacil Mayor y Alcaide de Toledo14歲，母親Teresa Bermey40歲。他與Gracia Gonzalbez在西班牙結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1208年11月11日在他的家鄉享年82歲，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多三 羅曼 德 洛斯 蒙特斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Esteban Illán de San Román Bermey de Toledo于大约1127年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多时，他的父亲Illán Pérez de San Román Alguacil Mayor y Alcaide de Toledo14岁，母亲Teresa Bermey40岁。他与Gracia Gonzalbez在西班牙结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1208年11月11日在他的家乡享年82岁，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多三 羅曼 德 洛斯 蒙特斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: San
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Khin, Pui, Sokhom, Heng, Kin, Kin Hong, Kun, Lai, Leong, Man, Ming, Ouk, Senh, Ly, Mui, Pha, Phal, Pham, Tha, Tran, Coong, Dam, Duy, Hanh, Noy, Hoeun, Noeun, Phath, Roeuth, Samoeun, Soeum, Sophat, Sovann, Yoeun, Pong, Savuth, Thai.
Burmese: from a personal name usually forming part of a compound name, from san ‘moon’, also ‘good luck’, or from a homonym meaning ‘spring (of water)’. See also 2 below.

Burmese: from a personal name usually forming part of a compound name, from hsan ‘to be extraordinary’. — Note: Since Burmese do not have hereditary surnames, these names (see also 1 above) were registered as such only after immigration of their bearers to the US.

variants.name: Shen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Wen, You Qin, Ning, Jian, Ying, Li, Wei, Yi, Fei, Hui, Min, Chang, Chung, Tian, You, Sa, Yiping, Yu-Min.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 沈: (i) from Shen (沈), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in present-day Shanxi province) prior to the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). (ii) from the placename Shen (沈), the name of a fief (located in Pingyu in present-day Henan province) granted to Ji Zai, also known as Dan Shu, the tenth son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056  BC ). After the state was annexed by the state of Cai in 506  BC , people kept the name of the fiefdom as their surname. (iii) from Shen Lu (沈鹿), the name of a fief (located in Hubei province) granted to Zhen, son of King Zhuang of Chu (ruler of the state of Chu, died 591  BC ). His descendants adopted part of the name of the fiefdom, 沈, as their surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 申, meaning ‘state, explain’ in Chinese: (i) from Shen (申), an ancient state (located in Ningxia and Shaanxi provinces), also known as West Shen. After it was annexed by the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people used the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from Shen (申), the name of a state (located in Nanyang in Henan province), also known as Southern Shen, granted to Shen Bo (申伯, the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After the state was annexed by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the placename Shen (申), the name of a fief (located in Nanyang in Henan province) granted to Shen Shu, an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) from the placename Shen (申), the name of a fief (located in Xiangcheng in Henan province) granted to Shen Hou (Marquis of Shen), an official in the state of Zheng (located in part of present-day Shaanxi and Henan province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Román
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Angel, Jorge, Miguel, Pedro, Francisco, Julio, Mario.

Spanish and Hungarian (Román); Catalan, French, English, Dutch, German, Romanian, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Ukrainian, Rusyn, Belorussian, and Croatian: from the Latin personal name Romanus, which originally meant ‘Roman’. This name was borne by several Christian saints, including a 7th-century bishop of Rouen, and was a baptismal name of the Russian prince Boris , who is venerated as a saint in the Orthodox Church. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of Slavic patronymics like Polish Romanowicz . Compare Romano .
Americanized form of German Romann: variant of 1 above or from the ancient Germanic personal name Hrodman (see Rodeman ).

variants.name: Romanic
meanings: Serbian and Croatian (Romanić): patronymic from the personal name Roman .

variants.name: Romanczuk
meanings: Polish: patronymic from the personal name   Roman  . Compare   Romanchuk  .

variants.name: Romanenko
meanings: Ukrainian: patronymic from the personal name Roman , formed with the diminutive suffix -enko.

variants.name: Romanek
meanings: Polish; Czech (Románek): from a pet form of the personal name Roman .

variants.name: Roma
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Gino, Antonio, Armando, Eduardo, Americo, Gaetano, Gerardo, Pasquale, Renato, Vito.  Spanish Jose, Fernando, Juan, Liduvina.
Italian: habitational name from the city of Rome (Italian Roma).
Italian: from the female or male personal name Roma ‘Rome’, attested since the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Romanko
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Galina, Natalya.
Ukrainian and Polish: from a pet form of the personal name   Roman  .

variants.name: Boris
meanings: Slovak: from the personal name Boris, which is often taken as a shortening of the Slavic personal name Borislav, composed of the elements bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se) + slav ‘glory’ or ‘famous’ (from Old Slavic slava ‘glory’), but more probably it is an altered form of the Old Bulgarian personal name Bogoris (a byname from Turkic bogori ‘small’), borne by the king of the Bulgars (sometimes known as Boris) who was converted to Christianity in 864. The name Boris was later also borne by a Russian prince, murdered in 1015, whose baptismal name was Roman and who is venerated as a saint in the Orthodox Church.
Altered form of Polish, Ukrainian, and Rusyn   Borys  , a cognate of 1 above.

Slovak (Boriš): from a short form of any of various Slavic compound personal names, containing the element bor ‘to fight’, e.g. Borislav (see 1 above). The homonymous Sorbian cognates, Boriš (Upper Sorbian) and Bóriš (Lower Sorbian), are found in Germanized and Americanized forms, only (see Borisch and Borish ).

variants.name: Romanik
meanings: Czech and Slovak (Romaník): from a pet form of the personal name Roman .

variants.name: Romain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Alain, Altagrace, Aurore, Camille, Dominique, Edele, Georges, Henri, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Jean-Michel, Jeanmarie.
French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and English: variant of   Roman  .

variants.name: Romano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Mario, Antonio, Carmine, Rocco, Sal, Vito, Pasquale, Gennaro, Alfonso, Gaetano, Santo, Vincenzo.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Rosario, Emilio, Juan, Miguel.

Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese: from the personal name Romano, Latin Romanus, borne by several Christian martyrs and saints. This was originally a byname for someone from Rome or for a Roman citizen, from Roma ‘Rome’. See also Roman .
Italian (Romanò): in Calabria a term for someone from Nea Rhōmē ‘New Rome’, a medieval Greek name for Constantinople; in Lombardy a habitational name from Romanò or Villa Romanò in Inverigo, Como.

nameMeanings.name: Bermey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Swiss German Bürgi (see Burgi ) and perhaps also of its cognate Bürki (see Burki ). Compare Berkey .

variants.name: Burgi
meanings: Italian: plural form of   Burgio   1.
Swiss German (Bürgi): from a pet form of the personal name Burkhard (see Burkhart ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Toledo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Jorge, Manuel, Ramon, Rafael, Juan, Antonio, Jesus, Miguel, Roberto, Carlos, Francisco.

Spanish and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from the city in central Spain, which was the capital of the Visigothic state between the 6th and 8th centuries. Its role declined for three centuries after the Muslim invasion of Spain, until it was taken as the capital of the kingdom of Castile between the 11th and 16th centuries. It was a major cultural and political center throughout the Middle Ages, and was also the home of an important Jewish community. The placename, first recorded in Latin as Toletum, is of obscure etymology, possibly connected with Toleto in Piedmont; Jewish tradition connects it with Hebrew toledot ‘generations’, but this is no more than folk etymology. Compare Toledano .

Jewish (Sephardic): also an adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the surname their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Toledano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Shlomo, Igal, Moshe, Shimon, Ehud, Shmuel, Yehuda.  Spanish Alberto, Gonzalo, Alina, Carlos, Conrado, Elena, Enrique, Evelia, Luis, Manuel, Miguel, Ramona.  French Gabrielle, Michel.
Spanish and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name for someone from Toledo (see   Toledo  ), from an adjectival form of the placename.

nameMeanings.name: Esteban
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek. See Stephen .

variants.name: Stephen
meanings: Usual English spelling of the name of the first Christian martyr (Acts 6–7), whose feast is accordingly celebrated next after Christ's own ( 26 December ). His name is derived from the Greek word stephanos ‘garland, crown’.

nameMeanings.name: Illán
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alan  , used mainly in Scotland and North America.

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Esteban Illán de San Román Bermey de Toledo was born about 1127, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, his father, Illán Pérez de San Román Alguacil Mayor y Alcaide de Toledo, was 14 and his mother, Teresa Bermey, was 40. He married Gracia Gonzalbez in Spain. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 11 November 1208, in his hometown, at the age of 82, and was buried in San Román de los Montes, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain.

fr.Parroquia San Román,Toledo,España: San Román de los Montes, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Esteban Illán de San Román Bermey de Toledo est né environ 1127, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, son père, Illán Pérez de San Román Alguacil Mayor y Alcaide de Toledo, avait 14 ans et sa mère, Teresa Bermey, avait 40 ans. Il a épousé Gracia Gonzalbez à Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 11 novembre 1208, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 82 ans, et a été enterré à San Román de los Montes, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne.

de.Parroquia San Román,Toledo,España: San Román de los Montes, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien
de.lifeSummary: Als Esteban Illán de San Román Bermey de Toledo um 1127 in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Illán Pérez de San Román Alguacil Mayor y Alcaide de Toledo 14 und seine Mutter Teresa Bermey 40. Er hat in Spanien Gracia Gonzalbez geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 11. November 1208 im Alter von 82 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in San Román de los Montes, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien begraben.

Lui sposato Gracia Gonzalbez nel Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 11 novembre 1208, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 82 anni, e fu sepolto a San Román de los Montes, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna.

ko.Parroquia San Román,Toledo,España: 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도 산 롬a 데 로스 몽스
ko.lifeSummary: Esteban Illán de San Román Bermey de Toledo(이)가 대략 1127년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Illán Pérez de San Román Alguacil Mayor y Alcaide de Toledo(은)는 14세였고, 그의 어머니 Teresa Bermey(은)는 40세였다. 그는 스페인에서 Gracia Gonzalbez(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1208년 11월 11일 그의 고향에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도 산 롬a 데 로스 몽스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Esteban Illán de San Román Bermey de Toledo nasceu aproximadamente 1127, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha, seu pai, Illán Pérez de San Román Alguacil Mayor y Alcaide de Toledo, tinha 14 anos e sua mãe, Teresa Bermey, tinha 40 anos. Ele casou-se com Gracia Gonzalbez em Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 11 de novembro de 1208, em sua cidade natal, com 82 anos, e foi sepultado em San Román de los Montes, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha.

ru.Parroquia San Román,Toledo,España: сан ромaн де лос монтесь, толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Esteban Illán de San Román Bermey de Toledo родился около 1127 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания, его отцу по имени Illán Pérez de San Román Alguacil Mayor y Alcaide de Toledo было 14 лет, а матери, которую звали Teresa Bermey, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Gracia Gonzalbez в местности Испания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 11 ноября 1208 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 82 лет и был похоронен в местности сан ромaн де лос монтесь, толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания.

es.Parroquia San Román,Toledo,España: San Román de los Montes, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Esteban Illán de San Román Bermey de Toledo nació aproximadamente 1127, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España, su papá, Illán Pérez de San Román Alguacil Mayor y Alcaide de Toledo, tenía 14 años y su mamá, Teresa Bermey, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Gracia Gonzalbez en España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 11 de noviembre de 1208, en su pueblo, a la edad de 82 años, y fue sepultado en San Román de los Montes, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España.
